title: Runner Envelope
description: Wrapper envelope emitted by the shared qwen-runner implementation (and its qwen-backed shims: glm, gemma, minimax). All --json responses conform to this contract.

runner.description: Requested runner identity (qwen, glm, gemma, minimax, ...).

effective_runner.description: Runner that actually produced output; equals runner unless a fallback occurred.

effective_model.description: Model that actually answered; equals model unless overridden by the CLI/provider.

effective_provider.description: Provider backing the effective runner (anthropic, openai, google, qwen, z-ai, moonshot, minimax, ...).

auth_ok.description: True on success, false when the CLI binary is missing or a fallback occurred, null when the run errored before auth could be established.

fallback_reason.description: Why a fallback was used, or null when no fallback occurred. Qwen-backed runners never fall back, so this stays null.

success.description: Whether the run completed with return_code 0 and no native error.

return_code.description: 0 success, -1 timeout, -2 CLI not found (seat_unavailable), -3 invalid input / native API or auth error / unexpected error.

stdout.description: Raw CLI stdout (native stream-json, json, or text). For debugging; prefer agent_message.

stderr.description: CLI stderr plus any native [API Error: ...] text appended by the wrapper.

command.description: Shell-quoted command line that was executed.

working_dir.description: Working directory the CLI ran in.

role.description: Role overlay applied (planner, codereviewer, implementer, synthesizer, adversarial, challenger, researcher), or null.

restrict_tools.description: Whether a read-only analysis overlay was applied.

agent_message.description: Clean final answer: trimmed result text for plain runs, or JSON-serialized output for --output-schema (structured_output) runs. Orchestrators should read this instead of parsing stdout.

session_id.description: Session id reported by the native stream, when available.

native_result.description: Raw result payload from the native result event. May be a string, object, or array; presence indicates the CLI emitted a result event. For --output-schema runs this is the structured object (agent_message is its JSON serialization).

status.description: Present only when return_code is -2, set to 'seat_unavailable'.

prompt_file.description: Present when exactly one --prompt-file was supplied.

prompt_files.description: Present when more than one --prompt-file was supplied.
